# Contributing

This project is optimized for self-directed contributors who are comfortable reading code, understanding intent, and acting without supervision.

If you’re here to use it, welcome.
If you’re here to improve it, even better.

## How this project works

- Pull requests are the default.
- Issues exist to document bugs, gaps, or edge cases.

Do not wait for permission to fix something. Just fix it.

## Fixing bugs

If you found a bug and you can fix it, open a pull request directly and explain the fix clearly.

If there is an existing issue, link it.
If there is no issue, the PR itself is sufficient.

You don’t need prior approval to submit a fix.
If you can do it, just do it.

## New features

Bug fixes and correctness improvements are always welcome. Scope expansion is evaluated cautiously.

## Pull requests

Pull requests must be:

- Clearly scoped
- Intentional
- CI-clean
- Justified

Justification means concrete grounding: specifications, RFCs, documented behavior, usage, or precise examples.

Changes motivated purely by taste, cleanliness, or abstraction alone are unlikely to be accepted.

## AI code

AI-assisted code is allowed and strongly encouraged.

AI can handle a large portion of the work. The CI exists to verify correctness and quality.

However:

- The contribution is treated as entirely yours.
- You are responsible for understanding what you submit.
- You are responsible for correctness, clarity, and documentation.
- Passing CI is required but not sufficient.

Low-quality, unreviewed, or blindly pasted output will be rejected.

## Communication boundaries

All technical discussion happens in public issues or pull requests, in a form others can inspect and act on.

As such, private messages, emails, or off-platform requests about bugs or features are ignored or flagged for spam.

## Forking

If this operating model matches how you work, contributions are welcome.

If not, you are free to fork the code and proceed independently.
